mnemonics: Mother Doesn't Want To Eat Desert Worms Raging Oceanically
Mike's Dogs Wander Through Rocky Hills Underneath Sand
keyPoints: A hierarchy of living organisms and non-living components
Types of ecosystems: Animal-plant community interacting with each other and their environment
Forest ecosystem: complex, diverse, and self-sustaining
Desert ecosystem: hot, dry, and often cold at night
Wetlands ecosystem: unique, diverse, and important for human life
Riverine ecosystem: constantly changing, influenced by water flow
Oceanic ecosystem: vast, diverse, and interconnected with other ecosystems
